title: Configuring Uniface to Use a Specific JRE
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on Microsoft Windows
Configuring Uniface to Use a Specific JRE
text: Configuring Uniface to Use a Specific JRE You can configure Uniface to use a different Java Runtime Environment (JRE) than the one selected during installation. To manually configure Uniface to use a specific JRE: Stop Uniface and the Tomcat web server, if they are running. On Windows, choose Web Server >Stop Tomcat Service from the Uniface program group. Edit the service.bat file located in UnifaceInstallDir /common/tomcat/bin , and set the JAVA_HOME variable to the location of your Java installation's JRE directory. For example: @echo off chcp 1252 set CATALINA_HOME=D:/Uniface10/common/tomcat ;set JAVA_HOME=D:/Uniface10/common set JAVA_HOME=C:/Progra~1/Java/jre1.6.0_12 Copy Specify the location of the jvm.dll in the [ USER_3GL] section of the usys.asn file, located in UnifaceInstallDir /common . For example: [USER_3GL] ;D:/Uniface10/common/jre/bin/client/jvm.dll(JNI_CreateJavaVM) C:/Progra~1/Java/jre1.6.0_12/bin/client/jvm.dll(JNI_CreateJavaVM) Copy Rerun services.bat to use the JRE. Restart Tomcat, then restart Uniface. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
